acceptance_targets: [CRAFT_CORRECTION_MAP_PAYOFF] Before the recording beat at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:519, the revised chapter must show Mallick using at least one named prior correction from the Saturday case to ask a question or request evidence that changes the live investigation. A reader should be able to point to a specific correction-map entry in the material around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:135 and a later resulting contradiction, suspect narrowing, or access inference elsewhere in the chapter without relying on Nate's phone.

[DIALOGUE_COMPOSED_WITNESS_DIAGNOSIS] In the witness material around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:229, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:303, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:351, at least two speakers must provide a concrete observed beat such as an exact phrase, a movement, an interruption, or a timed location shift. No more than one line in that cluster should summarize the whole Tommy-Cliff situation as an abstract diagnosis or quotable insight.

[PROSE_POV_INTERIORITY_DROP] The revised text must add at least one clearly internal Mallick beat in the collection sequence around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:493 and at least one clearly internal Mallick beat in the replay/interrogation sequence around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:579. Both additions must change how a reader understands her suspicion or tactical read of the room; merely describing external movement does not pass.

[PROSE_NEGATION_SUFFICIENCY_TIC] Across the passage clusters beginning at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:117 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:645, no more than one exact negation-plus-sufficiency construction may remain. The revised lines must still preserve three separate meanings by direct reading: the drowning inference, Tommy's visible crack, and Mallick's sub-legal certainty.

[CRAFT_ARNESON_PURE_FUNCTION] By the end of the material anchored at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:23 or snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:483, a cold reader must be able to name one Arneson-specific characteristic beyond size and competence. If the only available description after revision is still 'the deputy who does what Mallick says,' this finding remains unresolved.

findings.acceptance_test: Before the recording beat at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:519, the revised chapter must show Mallick using at least one named prior correction from the Saturday case to ask a question or request evidence that changes the live investigation. A reader should be able to point to a specific correction-map entry in the material around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:135 and a later resulting contradiction, suspect narrowing, or access inference elsewhere in the chapter without relying on Nate's phone.
findings.problem: The chapter announces Mallick's correction map as the operative method for this homicide, but on the page the map never does decisive work. She revisits the notebook and names Cliff's prior study knowledge as important, yet the actual turn arrives through Nate's recording, not through a concrete question, contradiction, or suspect narrowing generated by a specific Saturday correction. Because the context pack explicitly tracks this as a payoff to land in chapter 15, the current draft reads as gesturing at the payoff rather than dramatizing it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Strengthen the beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:135 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:159 by making one specific prior correction directly drive the next interview move before the audio reveal at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:519. A workable revision would use the study correction to force a question about access, paper, or room knowledge in one of the interview openings at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:283 or snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:337, so the notebook produces an actual investigative edge rather than a reminder.

findings.acceptance_test: In the witness material around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:229, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:303, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:351, at least two speakers must provide a concrete observed beat such as an exact phrase, a movement, an interruption, or a timed location shift. No more than one line in that cluster should summarize the whole Tommy-Cliff situation as an abstract diagnosis or quotable insight.
findings.problem: Three separate witnesses end up delivering the same polished diagnosis of the Tommy-Cliff dynamic in slightly different wording. Nate's 'Quiet weird. Not mad weird. Which is worse,' Joel's 'Cliff had leverage. Tommy knew it,' and Paul's 'The kind where one person already knows too much' are all concise mini-diagnoses rather than messy witness recollection. This is composed dialogue in the chapter's interview cluster: more mini-speech diagnosis and quotable compression than overheard speech, and it blurs the idiolect separation the style bible asks for.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the interview passages beginning at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:229, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:303, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:351 so that at least two of the three men give concrete observed behavior instead of abstract relationship diagnosis. Keep each man's voice profile intact by letting Nate over-describe and self-correct, Joel pin down a factual inconsistency, and Paul anchor his concern in time, placement, or risk language rather than all three converging on neat interpretive summaries.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised text must add at least one clearly internal Mallick beat in the collection sequence around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:493 and at least one clearly internal Mallick beat in the replay/interrogation sequence around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:579. Both additions must change how a reader understands her suspicion or tactical read of the room; merely describing external movement does not pass.
findings.problem: Once the BCA arrives and the phones hit the table, the chapter spends long stretches in action and transcript with almost no Mallick interiority. Because this chapter is locked to her close-third POV, the evidence sequence should carry her live analytic and sensory experience. Instead the key late beat often reads like external blocking and recorded dialogue, which makes the chapter's most important turn flatter and more procedural than the style bible's narrative-distance standard supports.
findings.rewrite_direction: Thread Mallick's interior response back into the late evidence sequence around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:483, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:509, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:579. Give the reader her physical or analytic reaction as the phones appear, as she notices the dinner file length, and as Tommy hears the playback, so the scene stays inside her processing rather than living only in room choreography and quoted audio.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the passage clusters beginning at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:117 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:645, no more than one exact negation-plus-sufficiency construction may remain. The revised lines must still preserve three separate meanings by direct reading: the drowning inference, Tommy's visible crack, and Mallick's sub-legal certainty.
findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same negation-then-sufficiency sentence scaffold at multiple hinge moments: not enough, but enough; not much, but enough; not big, but enough. One use can land. Repeated across the dock inference, Tommy's small cracks, and Mallick's end-of-scene certainty, the construction starts to read as an autopilot emphasis pattern rather than scene-specific prose.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence architecture at the beats anchored to snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:117, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:645, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:675, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:695. Keep the dramatic function of each moment, but replace at least two of the repeated enough/not-enough constructions with syntax or image that belongs to that local beat rather than to a reusable template.

findings.acceptance_test: By the end of the material anchored at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:23 or snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:483, a cold reader must be able to name one Arneson-specific characteristic beyond size and competence. If the only available description after revision is still 'the deputy who does what Mallick says,' this finding remains unresolved.
findings.problem: Arneson is on stage throughout the chapter with enough dialogue and procedural responsibility to register as a recurring presence, but he remains pure function: the same big competent deputy and nothing else. In a chapter this dependent on pressure inside the crime-scene machine, leaving Mallick's primary law-enforcement counterpart as procedural furniture thins the world around her and makes the civilian suspects feel more alive than the active investigation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Use Arneson's first entrance at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:23 or his evidence-handling beats at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:483 to give him one local trait, stress behavior, or phrasing habit that belongs to him. The fix should stay economical: a practical objection, a tired tell, a way of answering Mallick, or a specific reaction to the second death is enough if it marks him as more than a tool.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: The comedy is the creative risk and the creative achievement. These are men in their late forties who talk the way men in their late forties talk when there are no women present and the drinks have been flowing for hours: crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel, and occasionally accidentally beautiful. Do not clean this up. Do not soften the language. Do not make them witty where they would be vulgar. Do not make them eloquent where they would be clumsy. The profanity is not decoration — it is the texture of the friendship. When the second death breaks the comic register, the absence of that profane warmth should feel like a loss, not a relief. The murder, when it arrives, should be rendered with physical specificity — the water, the struggle, the sounds. The grief should be specific. The shame should be specific. The twenty-two-year-old guilt should be embodied, not summarized. If a scene makes the writer uncomfortable, that is a signal to stay in it longer, not to cut away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Every euphemism for death, sex, bodily function, emotional state, or financial desperation must be replaced with the direct term or a character-specific avoidance that itself reveals something about the character. Cliff saying 'restructure' when he means 'I'm broke' is character work. The narrator describing Cliff as 'facing financial challenges' is sanitization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the absolute norm. These are informal men in their late forties who have been friends for twenty-five years. Uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate emphasis, Joel's occasional legal precision, or Mallick's public-persona overcorrection. If two consecutive lines of dialogue contain uncontracted forms and neither character has a stated reason for formality, at least one is wrong.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. After a dialogue-heavy chapter, lead the next with interiority or description. After an interview chapter, give the reader a memory or a quiet observation. After a comic set piece, let the next chapter breathe. The scene counts vary (2-3) and should feel varied — a two-scene chapter after a three-scene chapter creates natural pacing shift. The tonal break at Chapter 14 should feel like a gear change, not a gradual dimming.
prose_style_profile.diction: Muscular, specific, unpretentious. No literary posturing. The prose should sound like intelligent conversation, not workshopped fiction. Prefer concrete nouns over abstract ones. Prefer active constructions. Avoid decorative metaphor — when a metaphor appears, it should be load-bearing. The comedy demands precision: funny is specific, and vague is never funny.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Prestige-literary inflation — do not elevate the comic register into literary solemnity. A man flushing Viagra while a detective knocks on the door is funny. The prose should be funny. Do not describe it as if it were a scene from Dostoevsky.

Comedic exhaustion — do not let every paragraph contain a joke. Comedy needs rhythm, which means it needs non-comic beats to land against. Let some paragraphs be straightforwardly descriptive or emotional.
Interior monologue sprawl — close third is not stream of consciousness. The POV character's interiority should be selective and purposeful, not an open channel of every thought.
Simultaneous narration — do not describe characters 'simultaneously' feeling multiple things. Choose the dominant feeling and let the undertone be implicit.
Forensic procedure porn — this is not a police procedural. Keep investigation details specific but brief. The comedy and character are the vehicle, not the forensics.

Metaphor creep — do not let the same metaphor template appear more than twice in a chapter. If you've used a sports metaphor, a water metaphor, and a fire metaphor, check that none of them are doing the same structural work.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV. Each chapter is locked to one character's interiority. No omniscient intrusions. The prose should take on the coloring of the POV character without becoming parody — Nate's chapters move faster and list more, Doug's chapters are quieter and more observational, Mallick's chapters shift between her two registers.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Conversational propulsion, not literary drift. The prose should move like someone telling you this story — not rushed, but never self-admiring. Vary sentence length aggressively: long compound sentences for the comic set pieces, short declarative hits for the turns. The humor lives in rhythm as much as content — the timing of a punchline is a prose-level decision.
sensory_bias: auditory — conversations, lake sounds, house creaks, the specific silence of a house where someone died
proprioceptive — hangovers, physical discomfort, adrenaline, the specific sensation of a body that hasn't slept
olfactory — booze, lake water, morning coffee, the basement must, the casserole
